regime_fall.current_status: Iran remains under severe military pressure from Israeli strikes targeting regime security institutions (MOIS, LEC) and US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, while internal divisions deepen as negotiating team head Ghalibaf steps down amid ceasefire disagreements and the regime projects military confidence despite acknowledged constraints, with Lebanon ceasefire linked to Hormuz reopening creating interconnected regional tensions.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 25-26, 2026, Iran's negotiating team head Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stepped down amid internal disagreements over ceasefire talks, while Israeli Defense Forces expanded strikes to target Iranian regime security institutions (MOIS and LEC headquarters) and Hezbollah launched attacks prompting Israeli counterstrikes and a 3-week ceasefire extension, marking a convergence of diplomatic breakdown, internal leadership fracture, and expanded military operations.
